output:
~8% revenue increase from the load time difference alone (0.8s × 1% per 0.1s = 8%).

This means roughly 40% of the observed 20% lift (8/20) could be attributable to the CDN speed advantage rather than the design itself. The real design effect may be closer to ~12%, or possibly less if speed and design interact non-linearly. The team should rerun the test with matched CDN configurations before attributing the full lift to Design B.
